danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile creatures and adversaries positioned throughout levels
danger_signals.meaning: Areas with enemies indicate danger zones requiring combat or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Dark, gothic visual presentation emphasized in level design
danger_signals.meaning: Overall darker tone communicates increased threat and hazardous environment compared to original game

danger_signals.visual_form: Three-act level design with escalating complexity
danger_signals.meaning: Longer, more complex levels with numerous secrets signal sustained danger and environmental threats

danger_signals.visual_form: Stars thrown at enemies, drill jump attacks required
danger_signals.meaning: Enemies require active engagement; their presence demands player action to proceed safely

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible power-up items scattered throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Power-ups provide temporary protection or enhanced abilities to mitigate danger
Resource gathering provides points and sustenance, indicating safe collection opportunities

safety_signals.visual_form: Ingeniously designed hidden passages and routes
safety_signals.meaning: Secrets offer alternative paths and safe havens away from main threat corridors

safety_signals.visual_form: Exit point at end of each act
safety_signals.meaning: Clear goal indicator provides safe progression milestone and level completion

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Museum of Horrors, ancient castle - player-accessible spaces

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ektor's henchmen, Zero the Kamikaze Squirrel
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Museum of Horrors environment, highest tower - enemy stronghold

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Various enemy types throughout eight worlds
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Scattered throughout all level acts as obstacles and threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ancient castle setting accessible via magician's box; darker gothic aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ektor survives via Zero's intervention, initiating 'Plan B'; enemies actively pursue player across eight worlds

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Seven worlds with three acts each, final world with unique structure; varied environmental designs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Escalating level complexity, longer level designs than original game, increased secret density

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark theme, enemies present in every level, hazardous platforming sections
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Constant combat requirements, evasion mechanics, strategic power-up placement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hidden letter placement spelling 'AERO', secret areas throughout levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Optional cup-switching mini-game accessible via letter collection; extra lives as reward

environmental_storytelling.example: Museum of Horrors setting establishes Ektor's lair and villain stronghold; magician's box teleportation reveals ancient castle as story progression
Zero the Kamikaze Squirrel appearing as Ektor's henchman throughout levels demonstrates ongoing antagonistic relationship and 'Plan B' execution
Longer, more complex level designs than original game visually communicate escalated conflict and sustained danger from antagonist forces
Hidden AERO letters scattered across acts provide environmental puzzle elements and discovery incentive, reinforcing exploration-focused level design
Dark gothic aesthetic maintained across worlds reinforces continuous threat state and maintains tonal consistency of antagonist's domain

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Performs drill jump attacks (diagonal up/down, straight down), collects food and stars, searches for hidden letters and secrets
Uses star projectiles and drill jump to defeat enemies; can target enemies directly below with new downward drill attack
Survives initial defeat via Zero's intervention; initiates contingency 'Plan B' to continue threat against Aero
Pursues Aero throughout levels as combat antagonist; demonstrates loyalty by rescuing Ektor and executing 'Plan B' orders
Present throughout level acts; engage player on contact; defeatable via star throws and drill jump attacks

notes: Aero the Acro-Bat 2 employs classic platformer visual communication through enemy placement, environmental design, and resource distribution. The 'dark theme' mentioned in reviews serves as primary world-state indicator, signaling elevated danger. Faction identity is implicit rather than explicit—no color-coded faction systems or symbolic markers are documented. Political situation is simplified to binary protagonist-vs-antagonist conflict. The game communicates world-state through level structure progression and environmental complexity rather than dynamic visual shifts. Secret areas and collectibles serve as visual incentives for thorough exploration.
